$schema_note: No secret values are present in this file. It records build-input identity and structure only.

APP_GIT_SHA.description: git rev-parse HEAD of the commit being built

SGF_ENGINE_SOURCE_COMMIT.description: sgf_engine vendor source commit, verified and vendored -- see sgf_engine/VENDORED_FROM.txt and sgf_engine/PROVENANCE_VERIFICATION.md

external_content_boundary.description: Deliberately excluded from the application image as of DEPLOY-GOV-2E. The image builds and starts successfully without any of these being present; app.py already degrades gracefully (404 per missing static file, empty puzzle list) rather than crashing.

entries.treatment: VERSIONED STATIC ARTIFACT, mounted read-only at runtime
entries.mount_contract: GO_ODYSSEY_LIVE_STATIC_ROOT env var (docker-compose.prod.yml), read via app.py's _serve_live_static_or_baked_subpath for the /assets/<path:subpath> route
entries.absent_behavior: individual asset files return 404; app process stays healthy

entries.treatment: VERSIONED STATIC ARTIFACT (optional marketing media), mounted read-only at runtime
entries.mount_contract: same GO_ODYSSEY_LIVE_STATIC_ROOT mechanism as assets/, via the /shorts/<filename> route
entries.absent_behavior: individual short files return 404; app process stays healthy

entries.treatment: VERSIONED CONTENT BASELINE + persistent runtime storage (admin-mutable via the question-management API)
entries.mount_contract: QUESTIONS_JSON_PATH env var (docker-compose.prod.yml), defaults to /app/data/questions.json inside the persistent go-data volume so admin edits survive container recreation
entries.absent_behavior: app.py's _load_questions()/_load_questions_fresh() already guard with os.path.exists() and return an empty list; puzzle-serving routes degrade, app process stays healthy

excluded_permanently.description: Removed from the Dockerfile and build manifest in DEPLOY-GOV-2E. Not a content-boundary/mount question -- these should never enter a production image at all.

entries.reason: USER DATA -- table inventory includes users, friendships, game_results, teacher_student, teacher_comments, share_links, and player_* progress/inventory tables. Not referenced by any sqlite3.connect() call in current app.py/scheduler.py/shadow_judging.py/grimoire_api.py/db.py -- PostgreSQL is the authoritative runtime database. Must never be committed to Git or baked into an image.
OBSOLETE -- confirmed unreferenced by current runtime code; its two tables (zones, grimoires) are already created directly in PostgreSQL by app.py (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS zones/grimoires).

INTERNAL QA evidence (rwd_batch* reports, tree_null_records, parse_fail_repair_report). Zero references anywhere in app.py/scheduler.py/shadow_judging.py. Never had a runtime purpose in a production image.

runtime_only_not_build_inputs.reason: mounted as a read-only bind volume in docker-compose.prod.yml; never COPYed into the image by the Dockerfile
external content mount serving assets/ and shorts/, see external_content_boundary above

runtime_dependency_provenance.source_status: local Git provenance -- not previously pushed to canonical origin (Graph A / Codex local branches only)
runtime_dependency_provenance.status: governed Git provenance outside sgf_engine: 77 recovered or curated runtime files plus the newly-authored shadow_event_storage.py module (78 files total)
runtime_dependency_provenance.note: Most curated HTML/JS files use each file's LATEST known Git commit, which does not always byte-match current Production exactly (e.g. cache-busting version-bump drift); this is documented per-file rather than silently copying Production bytes. Newly-authored governed modules record their first reachable byte-identical source commit. See deploy/runtime-source-provenance.json for exact per-file status.
